reviews.title: These Are Great With Only One Exception...

However I am purchasing my second packet and one packet for the AirPods for a buddy so they are worth the price.  However I'm going to use less than a drop of Elmer's Glue on each of my earbuds so they will stay on long.  Obviously the designers did not consider anyone was stupid enough to sleep in the earbuds but yes, I am that stupid.  ;-)

reviews.title: Cheap Buys Cheap
reviews.text: Not sure what I expected for so little, so shame on me. I'll tell you these made my 9-year-old's ears hurt. I'm not sure how they could cancel any noise, as there is no buffer. I tried the ones she put on and they are very uncomfortable. Returning.

reviews.title: Great product and great price.
reviews.text: We bought a pack of these for my son because he goes through headphones crazy fast; since we bought these, he's still on the first pair. We will be buying more in the future if we have to.

reviews.title: They are priced low enough that a defective or marginal one doesn't sour the price.
reviews.text: Quality control could be a little better, but the price for the multipack is tough to beat.

reviews.title: For the cost… I am super happy with them.
reviews.text: I’m giving these earbuds five stars because they exceed my expectations when they only cost seventy cents each. I find it hard to complain about these, especially considering the cost. The sound is quite adequate for such earbuds. I use these in a classroom setting for kids who either cannot afford earbuds or forget there is at home. They are individually packaged. Nice assortment of colors. Though they are being sold by a US company, I do not show where they are made. I can only assume they are made in China.

reviews.title: Great Sound Just Wish they had a Microphone
reviews.text: Fun colors and love this type of ear plug over the cone shaped style. Listening to music on my phone sounds great  but I wish they had a microphone so I could easily answer calls. Now I have to unplug the earphones when the phone starts to ring and then re-plug after I'm done on the call. I guess they are really designed for use on exclusive music players. The listing is confusing because it says "Bluetooth" which they don't have, but the wire is about 4ft. long so your music unit can easily be kept anywhere convenient. No volume control either so that has to be handled on your phone/music player.

reviews.title: Exact type of ear buds I need.
reviews.text: These ear buds are hard to find anymore.  I love the ability to pull one bud out and leave it dangle vs the newer ear buds that you have to pocket if you take out.  I wish they had gel pieces that enter your ears as I have small ears so they get a little sore after several hours of wearing.  But I will take it as I can get the ease of use I want from them.  Volume, sound and use of buttons are all fantastic.

reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: I'm not keen on the cover material
reviews.text:
While the Arae Electronic Organizer does what it says, its implementation isn't great.

The double layers of this product has one and a half flap of elastic bands to hold bundled up wires, that's 18 spots total. The rest are two large pockets and a small pocket. That's plenty of room to carry most charging accessories that you'd have with you if you were equipped with tablets and phones. For laptops and cameras, you'll need other solutions. Even action camera chargers may have a hard time fitting in this slim product.

It's not a problem that this thing is pretty much made out of nylon from the outside in but the material that makes up the exterior of this product makes it look cheap. It's a rough, synthetic material that just makes it a bit cheap looking. I think cloth or leather would have been a better choice.

In addition, the zipper sticks, making it hard to quickly open this up to retrieve your wires for charging.

reviews.title: Case closed
reviews.text:
It's pretty amazing how much this will hold and still close up properly. I am using it for many cables and some adapters, and I am also keeping those little instruction manuals that come with everything in one of the zipper pouches. You can keep a lot of cables and adapters in this, and it will still zip up and be small enough for travel.
I like this case a lot.

reviews.title: Smaller than expected
reviews.text: Water proof? Not going to try that. This is super slim so you can't really fit a whole lot in. Wires, maybe... but devices, like portable routers, not exactly. Slim portable chargers, sure. Higher capacity batteries, nope. Won't fit anything more than two iPhones thick.

reviews.title: No More Ziplock Bags
reviews.text: This is such a better alternative to tossing cables into a ziplock bag.  The nice bag with zipper allows me to keep everything in one place.  I wished the compartments were a little wider, but it's easy to just use two compartments. Nice quality bag, once I get spares I would just pack these with a spare ready and be ready to go anytime.  Nice organization addition to my travel gear.

reviews.title: Handy little case
reviews.text: Does a good job at cutting down on cable clutter. I took a black Sharpie and identified the various cords by writing abbreviations on the cloth stretch band that secures the cords. It also helps organize miscellaneous adapters and charging units. Not sure how long it will last but for organizing cords and small electronic items it’s a good buy.

reviews.title: Nice little bud case
reviews.text: I could imagine a few other ways to utilize this little zippered case, designed for earbuds. But, it works very well for it's intended purpose. Even for some styles that have the 'wrap around' type support, as shown in my pics. It was a little snug, but they fit. The standard buds (either wired or wireless) fit much better. The case itself is nice 'pleather', good zipper and nicely appointed swivel snap to attach it to whatever you want to hang it on. My wife chose the natural leather color to go with one particular purse. She hangs it on the outside of the purse, like a 'bling' effect. Looks great. Good purchase. Five stars.

These fit perfectly in my briefcase and there is plenty of room for my over-sized buds. It's a really  attractive case  for the money. I'm not sure if that is real leather, but who cares as long as they protect  what's inside. The added hook comes in handy since I can attach it to a loop in my briefcase so that it doesn't sink to the bottom and I have to dig for it.

reviews.title: Good value for the price
reviews.text:
First I like sound! I'm an audiophile at heart but in my life now I'm an audiophile on a BUDGET! These are my first bluetooth speakers. More on that in a minute.

I was looking for on ear bluetooth speakers with a budget of about $50 to $60 and was set on waiting for a sale on the JBL EB45T but then saw these recommended on the wirecutter website (a pretty good review site in my opinion)

There is nothing wrong with these, but nothing really right about them either. They have a nice neutral sound that you can listen to for a long time. For movies and youtube on my computer, they are great. But there is nothing in the sound that is going to wow you either. Trust me if there was something actually wrong with the sound, I'd be telling you. You can listen to these for a long time without ear fatigue. If you are looking for lots of rumble for movies, these probably aren't the right phones for you. But if your movie listening requirements are more modest then these work well for that. For casual background music, podcasts, audiobooks, they will work really well for that.

Isolation is not great. I think the plastic is thin so a certain amount of noise gets in. I wanted to be able to hear a little bit of background noise, things going on in the house, but these push my limit for how much I want to hear. Even though they were open-back my Sennheiser HD-229 did a better job with isolation because of the design of the pad.

Comfort isn't bad and for my ears, the ear cup size is just about right size. I wish the earpieces swiveled a bit more but for my head, they swivel just enough. . They have a left and right and the cups swivel so little that if you have them on the wrong way you will notice.

Bluetooth... These are a pain in the backside on Win 7. When I want to use them I have to go and delete the previous pairing, pair them again and then go to the control panel and set these to be used for output. For movies on the computer, they get out of sync. I even set my movie player (Pot Player which I highly recommend) to use the Bluetooth headphones and they still get out of sync with the audio. I don't know if it's the player, the USB Bluetooth adapter, Windows 7 or some combination of them together.

I like that they can be used with a wire as well as with bluetooth. The controls are a little small, as mentioned by others but I don't have trouble working them.

I also tried the JBL EB45T Bluetooth headphones, which cost almost twice as much, at Target and these sound better overall. The JBL might have had better highs, a little punchier bass but these are more cohesive across the frequency range with nothing tweaked to make them sound 'better'.

Summary: I don't have any other Bluetooth headphones to compare these to but I think most people would be very satisfied with these at this price point for general use. If however, you are looking for audiophile on a budget, it's not here.

reviews.title: The sound quality is fantastic and the battery life is excellent
reviews.text: I've been very impressed by many things in the month that I've had this.  The sound quality is fantastic and the battery life is excellent.  I've owned these for a month and the leather cover over the ear cushion is splitting exposing the foam.  Not sure if this is a fluke or what kind of flaw this is.  I've just been using them at my desk and walking around town, medium use for about 5 weeks, when I noticed it was splitting.

reviews.text: Fantastic value. Amazing sound, light and comfortable. I never write reviews but I was so pleased with this product that I did.

reviews.title: Meh... just OK.  Not worth $100
reviews.text: Read some good reviews and picked it up on sale during prime sale.  To me, these are just OK.  I'm not overly impressed.  For $100 they are not worth it, less than $60, just OK.  I have a pair of motorola solo wireless and those are much better as it pertains to battery life, sound, and durability.  These seem like they are going to break very easily.  They feel extremely cheap.  The sound is not bad but nothing overly impressive.  Battery life is meh, it's giving low battery warnings at 50% levels based on what is reported to my iPhone, Plays that warning message about every 5 minutes or so... stupid.  When I turn it on it takes a bit for it to function with my iPhone 7, Motorola solo pretty much instantly ready.  On a positive note, I've used them with phone calls and no one complains... my Motorola solo there are a few times where people had issues hearing me.

reviews.title: [review for non ANC version] Pretty good deal for it's money
reviews.text:
I lost accurate track of time, but it was definitely more than 60hr of battery life. very impressive, a little scary to carry this much power on my head, but logically I understand that risks of catching fire or batteries exploding is negligible)

* sound quality - awesome (in terms of sound itself)
* build and material quality - impressive
* bluetooth connectivity - awesome. range, stability, reconnect speed, sound quality (in terms of losses via bluetooth while in varying range of "hotspot with music").
* battery quality - a little bit over a week of daily uses for 8hr+ a day. just perfect. That will be enough for a business trip out of town for a couple of days and back without need to recharge and sometimes even without prior notice to recharge before I go.
* distinct controls (bumps with space between them) - awesome. easy to use, easy to locate, no mistakes)
* folding capabilities - they do fold/unfold, but I tried it only once upon unpacking, but I rarely use this feature

The only minus that I can see in the last 20 days is how the headband is stretching when I'm wearing headphones. Looks dangerous (in terms of longevity). That's all I think. If this headphones will last more than a year I would call them perfect for a price and capabilities (tiny note about non ANC version and that's it)

So final score (while they last) 10/10

reviews.title: It is not passive soundproofing it is active. Which is NOT what I need.
reviews.text:
I have difficulty finding headphones with passive noise canceling for whatever reason and so I saw that this listing said passive and it was from the same brand as one I am still using. (It's breaking down due to age)

Active soundproofing is extremely detrimental to someone with audio disorders like myself since it makes a low buzzing noise that I can't tune out.

It does work as headphones but I would like to have it refunded so I can get another pair with the format of soundproofing I need.

Comfort & quality: padded pivoting ear cushions and an adjustable headband allows for a comfortable custom fit.
Hassle free packaging: no hard plastics, no scissors needed, enjoy opening your box frustration free.

reviews.title: They are ok but pricey for the quality
reviews.text: They are ok but pricey for the quality.
